ground_truth.title: Allure Best of Beauty Awards 2025: Skin-Care Winners
ground_truth.author: Sophia Panych
ground_truth.main_content:
When Best of Beauty launched in 1996, skin care was something best kept hidden behind your medicine cabinet. Passed down from your mom or grandmother, it wasn’t something many discussed openly, and there was certainly no social media where you could share recommendations or research ingredients among fellow beauty lovers. Fast-forward to 2025, and we live in a world where tweens are familiar with niacinamide and LED masks are discussed at dinner parties. And while it’s great that we’re all a lot more open about what we put on our faces—and why we put it there—we also face a challenge: too much information and too many options can make finding a product that actually works and suits your skin type feel like a Herculean task.

Enter the 2025 Allure Best of Beauty Awards. The Skin category winners are probably the most difficult to choose and definitely the most hotly debated, but after months of thorough testing (and retesting), in the end, our editors came to a consensus on which cleansers, creams, peels, and spot treatments offer the most impressive results. And whether your skin’s dry or dehydrated, your acne’s hormonal or bacterial, you’ve got dark spots or dark circles, this list has options for all, reflecting the diversity in age, skin tone, and skin type of our tester pool.

This year, you’ll discover exciting new launches among longtime favorites (looking at you, Differin Adapalene Gel 0.1%). We’ve got masks developed by dermatologists, peels engineered by aestheticians, and buzzy K-beauty imports on the list. So scroll through and prepare to find some new additions to your routine that you’ll want to shout about from the rooftops—although your Instagram Stories will also suffice.

Best of Beauty Awards 2025: Skin Care
Shop your favorite category or keep scrolling for the year's very best face creams, cleansers, and serums.

See our winners:
- Best Face Cleansers
- Best Face Mist
- Best Face Scrubs
- Best Essence
- Best Exfoliating Toner
- Best Face Serums
- Best Acne Treatments
- Best Eye Creams
- Best Moisturizers
- Best Face Oil
- Best Face Masks
- Best Face Sunscreens
- Best Face Peel
- Best Hair-Removal Products
- Best Skin-Care Devices

Best Face Cleansers

Best Oil Cleanser: Ma:nyo Pure Cleansing Oil
Why It Won: We see your hesitations about using a cleansing oil (greasy film, clogged pores), and raise you a bottle of Ma:nyo Pure Cleansing Oil. This K-beauty favorite transforms from a silky oil into a luxurious milk that cleans out pores and removes every type of makeup imaginable, without replacing it with a greasy film. Among its 14 plant oils are argan kernel and rice bran, which regulate sebum production and loosen dead skin cells, respectively—and, together, leave your pores looking significantly smaller and your skin significantly smoother.
Tester feedback from commerce writer Lily Wohlner: “By the time I’m ready to take off my makeup, I’ve usually had it on all day—so the moment I walk through the door, it needs to come off fast and easily. This cleansing oil melts off my makeup in just a few swipes without irritating my acne-prone skin, and although it’s fragrance-free, the natural scent from the oils gives off a subtle, spa-like vibe that makes for the perfect way to unwind after a long day out and about.” —Lily Wohlner, commerce writer

Best Cleansing Balm: Dr. Althea Pure Grinding Cleansing Balm
Why It Won: Much of the buzz around the Dr. Althea Pure Grinding Cleansing Balm began around the packaging—an innovative twist dispenser that works like a pencil sharpener (remember pencils?) to shave off as much balm as you need to get cleansing. But that’s not the reason this took home the top spot. That’s down to the formula’s impressive makeup-melting, skin-soothing abilities, courtesy of nourishing oils (camellia sinensis and grape seed) and calming plant extracts (three forms of cica: madecassoside, Centella asiatica extract, and asiaticoside).
Tester feedback from associate beauty director Sarah Kinonen: “I don’t know what’s more fun: dispensing this cleanser with its twist top or watching it melt off all the makeup on my face in mere seconds.” —Sarah Kinonen, associate beauty director

Best Gel Cleanser: Rose Ingleton MD SuperFruit Brightening Cleanser
Why It Won: The Rose Ingleton MD SuperFruit Brightening Cleanser is exactly what you want from your daily face wash: a thorough, deep clean with a delicate touch. In addition to gentle, plant-derived surfactants, the gel-to-foam formula combines exfoliating sugarcane extract with hydrating hyaluronic acid that, when rinsed away, reveals skin that’s radiant and balanced—not rough and bothered.
Tester feedback from editor in chief Jessica Cruel: “Typically, I say cleanser is a throwaway step—something you don't need to spend too much energy worrying about, but after trying this one, I’ve changed my opinion. It doesn't leave skin feeling tight like most gel formulas and has a delicate exfoliant (sugarcane extract), making it gentle enough to use daily. I like to use it as the second step in a double-cleansing routine to remove sunscreen and New York City grime.” —Jessica Cruel, editor in chief

Best Cream Cleanser: Drunk Elephant Mello Marula Cream Cleanser
Why It Won: Grab your softest bathrobe and plushiest slippers because comfort is the name of the game when you wash your face with Drunk Elephant Mello Marula Cream Cleanser. Coconut oil-derived surfactants sweep away all manners of makeup (even the water-resistant kind) without stripping skin of moisture, as colloidal oatmeal soothes and marula seed oil gives you a hydrating head start on the rest of your skin-care routine.
Tester feedback from contributing writer Sophia Panych: “Oh, how I absolutely adore this cleanser—it makes me look forward to washing my face at night. Our skin feels so clean yet so comfortable, and I appreciate how effective it is at getting rid of all my eye makeup. I never find myself rubbing my lids or following up with micellar water to get rid of smudges under my lash lines.” —Sophia Panych, contributing writer

Best Face Mist

Weleda Skin Food Hydrating Face Mist
Why It Won: The only issue we have with the Weleda Skin Food Hydrating Face Mist is that it took the brand so long to launch it. For decades, makeup artists (and Allure editors, and a large cohort of models and celebrities) have relied on the Skin Food Original Ultra-Rich Cream to soothe and hydrate stressed complexions. Formulated with hyaluronic acid, glycerin, and the same botanical blend (calendula, chamomile, rosemary, pansy, and sunflower seed oil) as the cream, this milky mist refreshes our dull, thirsty skin during hot days cold snaps, and 3 p.m. slumps.
Tester feedback from managing editor Alessandra Foresto: “The Skin Food Original Ultra-Rich cream has been in my eczema-soothing kit since a makeup artist once used it on me back in the aughts. But as an everyday makeup-wearer, reapplying it as my skin got dry and flaky throughout the day has not been a practical option. This face-mist version lets me do just that. The formula is on the creamy side, so it really feels like liquid lotion. It leaves my skin soft and dewy, and has that classic Weleda Skin Food calendula and chamomile scent that I’ve always loved.” —Alessandra Foresto, managing editor

Best Treatment Facial Mist / Best Facial Mist With Hypochlorous Acid: Prequel Universal Skin Solution Dermal Spray
Why It Won: If you’re in the market for a face mist with hypochlorous acid—the multitasking, antimicrobial ingredient dermatologists love for reducing inflammation, calming irritation, and keeping bacteria on the skin in check—Prequel Universal Skin Solution Dermal Spray is the one to buy. It’s a simple, single-ingredient formula that’s gentle enough to use on even the most sensitive skin, and it’s a godsend for anyone dealing with acne, eczema, or rosacea. It’s also a great way to refresh your skin after a workout or a long day of wearing makeup.
Tester feedback from associate beauty director Sarah Kinonen: “I keep this in my bag at all times. It’s my go-to for calming down any redness or irritation, and it’s also great for setting makeup. It’s a true multitasker.” —Sarah Kinonen, associate beauty director
